bids.reasoning: At the start of the negotiation, I am targeting maximum utility because there is no time pressure to concede yet. With no opponent offers or messages, I have no evidence about their priorities, so I will open with my highest-value bid across all issues.

I am targeting a very high utility bid (about 0.973) because it is still the very start of the negotiation and there is no need to make large concessions yet. The opponent’s only offer was all valueA, which is the worst possible bid for my preferences and shows no real concession pattern yet. I will make a small concession on my lowest-weight issueA while holding firm on the more important issues.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.95 because it is still very early in the negotiation and there is no need to make major concessions yet. The opponent has repeated the all-valueA offer, which is very poor for me and shows no concession so far, so I will only concede on my lowest-weight issueA while holding firm on the more important issues B, C, and D.

I am targeting a high utility bid around 0.92 by conceding only on the least important issue, issueA, while holding firm on issueB, issueC, and especially issueD. Since it is still very early at t=0.06, there is no need for major concessions; the opponent has barely moved, only improving issueD from valueA to valueB, so I should continue pressing for strong terms.

I am targeting a high utility bid around 0.866 because it is still very early, so I should not concede much beyond low-weight issues. The opponent has mostly held at valueA for everything, with only small movements on issueC/issueD, so I will concede further on my lower-weight issueB while holding firm on the more important issueC and issueD.

I am targeting a high utility around 0.81 because it is still very early (t=0.10), so there is no need to make major concessions yet. The opponent has only moved slightly from all valueA offers, with small concessions on issueD, issueC, and issueB, so I will concede further on lower-weight issueB while holding firm on the more important issueC and issueD.

I am targeting a still-high utility bid (about 0.76) because it is early in the negotiation, so I should not concede much on my most important issues. The opponent has moved only slightly from all-valueA toward valueB on issueC/issueD, so I will concede on my lower-weight issueB while holding issueC and issueD at my best value. No messages were provided, so I rely only on the offer pattern.

At t=0.14 it is still early, so I am preserving my highest-weight issueD at valueD while making a measured concession on issueC. The opponent’s offers remain very low for my utility and show only minor concessions, so I am not matching their latest bid but am moving from my prior position to improve acceptability.

I am targeting a utility around 0.70, keeping firm on the highest-weight issueD and still holding issueC at a strong value because it is also important. The opponent’s latest offer finally conceded issueD to valueD, so I am preserving that while making concessions on lower-weight issueA/issueB; time is still early, so I do not need to drop near their low offers yet.

I am targeting a utility around 0.73, still high because it is early at t=0.18 and there is no need to concede heavily yet. The opponent has mostly held very low offers for me, though they have shown some movement on issueC and issueD; I will reward movement on issueC while holding firm on the most important issueD and making tradeoffs on lower-weight issues.

I am targeting a utility around 0.68, conceding fully on the lower-weight issues A and B while holding firm on issueD, my most important issue. At t=0.20 it is still early enough not to accept the opponent’s low offers, but their recent bids show some willingness to move on issueC and issueD, so I am combining those signals into a plausible compromise.

I am targeting a utility around 0.60, which is a meaningful concession from my earlier offers while still protecting my highest-weight issueD. At t=0.22 there is still time, so I should not drop issueD yet; the opponent’s offers show some movement but remain inconsistent and mostly low-value for me, with occasional concessions on issueC/issueD.
